instruction: Please help. I have an adult patient who has had many watery stools today. They are very restless and irritable, their eyes look sunken, and their mouth feels very dry. They are drinking the fluids very quickly and eagerly.

instruction: Field situation: A mother arrived with a baby who is vomiting and has very loose stools. She is very worried. What should I tell her? (Please include Swahili instructions for her).

instruction: A mother brings her 4-month-old baby to my station. She says the baby is breathing very fast and making a grunting sound every time he exhales. The baby's chest seems to be pulling in deeply under the ribs.
output: {"urgency": "IMMEDIATE", "situation_summary": "Infant presenting with rapid breathing, chest indrawing, and audible grunting.", "containment_check": null, "immediate_actions": ["Count the breaths for a full 60 seconds to confirm rate", "Check for nasal flaring during breathing", "Administer amoxicillin 40mg per kg twice daily", "Kaa tuliza mtoto (Keep the child calm)"], "do_not": ["Do not give any cough suppressants", "Do not delay referral if chest indrawing is present"], "escalate_if": ["Child shows severe chest indrawing", "Child is unable to drink or feed"], "confidence": "HIGH", "source": "RESPIRATORY DISTRESS IN CHILDREN"}

instruction: Field situation: I found a man in the sun, he is very hot to the touch, acting very confused, and seems to be losing consciousness. What should I do?

instruction: A community health worker is conducting a home visit. They find a child who has been recovering from a period of severe weight loss. The child is now active, has no swelling in the legs, and the MUAC is 11.8cm. The parent asks how much food to give now.

instruction: Field situation: The solar-powered refrigerator used for storing vaccines has stopped working due to heavy cloud cover, and the health worker needs to report the resource shortage.
output: {"urgency": "IMMEDIATE", "situation_summary": "Failure of temperature-controlled storage equipment for routine medical supplies.", "containment_check": null, "immediate_actions": ["Record the exact date and time the temperature deviation was noticed", "Note the current GPS coordinates of the health post", "List the current resources on hand and the specific resources needed", "Rank the needs by priority in the incident report"], "do_not": ["Do not leave the vaccines in the warm refrigerator", "Do not wait until the next scheduled report to communicate the failure"], "escalate_if": ["If the internal temperature exceeds the safe threshold for more than 30 minutes", "If there is no immediate alternative cooling method available"], "confidence": "HIGH", "source": "capacity needed / COMMUNICATION AND REPORTING"}

instruction: Please help. A mother brings her 3-year-old son to me. He has been having very watery stools for two days and looks very weak. I am worried about others in the village getting sick.

instruction: URGENT: I am at a new camp site and I've noticed several children under 5 are very thin, lethargic, and their skin looks loose. What should I do?

instruction: Field situation: A mother brings her 14-month-old child to the clinic. The baby is very lethargic, has swollen feet that stay indented when pressed, and the mid-upper arm circumference (MUAC) measurement is 10.2cm. The mother is crying and asks what to do.

instruction: I found a man lying in the road after a motorbike accident. He is unconscious, his skin feels very cold and sweaty, and his breathing is very fast. I can't find a strong pulse.

instruction: Need guidance now. A 3-year-old child was brought to the clinic with very watery stools for two days. The child is extremely lethargic, has sunken eyes, and when I pinch the skin on the stomach, it stays up for a long time. The child is too weak to swallow anything.

instruction: Responder query: I found an adult man lying on the ground in the market. He is not breathing and he is not responding when I shake him. What do I do?

instruction: A mother brings her 3-year-old son to the clinic. He has been very hot to the touch for two days and is acting very sleepy. The mother says, 'Mtoto ana homa kali sana' (The child has a very high fever). The child's temperature is 39.5°C.

instruction: A child accidentally drank some kerosene while playing. The child is awake and alert, and it happened about 20 minutes ago. I want to make the child vomit to get it out.

instruction: A mother brings her child to the clinic. She says the water from the local stream is very muddy after the rains and she wants to know how to make it safe for the baby to drink. She asks, 'Je peux utiliser du chlore pour nettoyer l'eau?'
